_doc: Per-language RESTRICTION to specific editions, for languages with enough distinct versions that pooling everything (onboard.py's default) would be noisy/redundant rather than helpful. A language absent from `editions` is unaffected — onboard.py pools every fetchable edition catalog_source.all_versions() finds. Present here, it pools ONLY the listed editions. Each entry: {source: pkf|helloao|dbt, param: the value that source's adapter needs (helloAO translation id / DBT bible_id — pkf just uses the iso), edition_code: the catalog's own identifier for it (used to derive a stable per-edition tag; see onboard.py's _tag())}. The FIRST entry in the list is the primary edition (becomes the published partition's driving alignment tag); the rest are pooled. Adding/removing/reordering entries here is safe and incremental — see onboard.py's module docstring for exactly what re-runs (the changed edition only) vs what's untouched (everyone else's cached ingest+align data) when you edit a list.

spa.note: canonical anchor — grouped with dbt:SPNR02 as the same text; our currently-published edition
unclassified (no likely verdict yet either way — grouped with dbt:SPAWTC as the same text; kept pending the producer's next fingerprinting pass rather than assumed redundant

por.note: anchor — canonical João Ferreira de Almeida, Brazilian (vname confirms); matches the Clear-gold benchmark edition for por
probably European (no Brasil tag anywhere); distinct_translation (0.69 vs PORFAA)

probably European (no Brasil tag); but textually close to the Brazilian Almeida cluster (0.85-0.86) — likely the historical Portugal-side Almeida revision, not a coincidence, since Almeida has both national lines
probably European (no country tag); distinct_translation (0.68 vs PORNLH); NT-only
probably European (no country tag); distinct_translation (0.73 NT / 0.78 OT)
probably European (no country tag); distinct_translation (0.52 vs PORNLH — the most independent of all por editions); NT-only

eng.note: English Standard Version — major, unclassified but independently significant
New American Standard Bible — flagged dialect_variant vs WEB (0.88) but kept for its stature
New Living Translation — major, unclassified but independently significant
World English Bible — major public-domain translation
King James Version (Cambridge Paragraph — catalog's own pick within the KJV group)
New International Version — MANUALLY ADDED; absent from the fingerprint graph entirely (not in any catalog-overlap.json entry), included on its own significance
distinct_translation (Wycliffe Modern, 0.57 vs KJV — the most independent of the historical-English cluster)

fra.note: anchor — existing published edition (LSG, Louis Segond 1910). FRALSG (dbt) went 404 (removed/renamed upstream on DBP after the catalog snapshot); FRNTLS was tried as a substitute but its own fingerprint pointed elsewhere (FRNO96/Ostervald) at the time. The catalog's 2026-07-23 update grouped FRALSG+FRNTLS+fra_lsg as the SAME text with fra_lsg as its own recommended default — confirms fra_lsg was the right pick, FRNTLS is NOT pooled separately below (would double-count the same translation).

dbt:FRADBY (also 'Darby') dropped from this list — it's the same historical Darby translation as fra_jnd (0.996 near-identical), just hosted by DBT instead of helloAO; pooling both would double-count it. FRNO96 (dbt) also dropped — already same-text-grouped with fra_ost.

hin.note: HINBIB, HINDPI, HINOHC dropped — regenerated from the fixed default auto-derivation (near-duplicate + testament-consistency fixes in onboard.py's _drop_near_duplicates()), each classified dialect_variant/orthography_convention/near_identical against something else in this list.

ind.note: dbt:INDASV dropped — dialect_variant (0.91, OT) of this edition; also helloao:ind_obo dropped entirely (same text as the PKF anchor, was slipping through as a separate entry due to a testament-inconsistent same_text_as flag — fixed in onboard.py).

ben.note: dbt:BNGDIP dropped — near_identical (0.98) to this edition. Reverses an earlier per-language call to keep both as a 'cross-edition agreement signal'; the new DEFAULT policy (near-duplicates drop uniformly) takes precedence for consistency across languages.

eng.reason: LXX-based (Greek-OT-sourced) English editions and dialect-specific editions (e.g. Aboriginal English) are deliberately excluded from the main eng pool — each needs its own dedicated pipeline/track rather than folding into the main-language partition. Listed here so they aren't silently forgotten, not because they're redundant.

por.reason: PTGLPF (Nova Versão Internacional 2011) has no text fileset on DBP at all — video_stream only (live-verified). It was originally included on prior knowledge of the translation's significance, but the 2026-07-23 catalog update dropped it from the fingerprint graph entirely, and it's still confirmed video-only live. Permanently unfetchable via dbt_source — not worth a wasted API call every onboarding run.

ben.reason: Both confirmed audio_drama/video_stream only on DBP, live-verified — no text fileset exists. BENBIB was also dropped from the catalog's fingerprint graph entirely (2026-07-23 update); BENCLV never carried a `likely` classification either (an unclassified singleton, same pattern). Kept out of the main ben pool for the same reason as por's PTGLPF.
